stepAnswer: Reject the null if q is outside the 99% CI; otherwise fail to reject.
stepTitle: D) Ciara now decides to perform a hypothesis test, with the null hypothesis that the true IQR is q.
stepBody: i. How could Ciara perform this using her confidence intervals? Discuss the duality of confidence intervals and hypothesis testing.
choices: Reject the null if q is outside the 99% CI; otherwise fail to reject.
Reject the null if q is inside the 99% CI.
Always reject the null when using confidence intervals.
Accept the null if q is greater than the sample IQR.
